title: lichen_l2_send net_pkt_unref on success may double-free if caller also unrefs
description:
In lichen_l2.c lichen_l2_send() lines 218-222:

    /*
     * Per Zephyr net_l2 contract: when L2 returns 0, it took ownership
     * of the packet and must free it.
     */
    net_pkt_unref(pkt);
    return 0;

The comment correctly states the Zephyr L2 contract. However, if a future maintainer doesn't understand this contract and adds an unref at a call site, or if the L2 contract changes in a Zephyr version upgrade, this could cause double-free.

The code is currently correct. This is a documentation/footgun issue:
1. The contract is only documented in this one comment
2. A contributor modifying lichen_l2_send() error paths might add cleanup code that unrefs on success
3. The asymmetry (error paths don't unref, success path does) is non-obvious

Consider: Adding a header comment to lichen_l2_send() declaration in lichen_l2.h explaining the ownership semantics.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c lines 218-222
close_reason: Documented net_pkt ownership semantics in both lichen_l2.h (module-level comment explaining TX/RX ownership contract) and lichen_l2.c (lichen_l2_send function docstring). On success L2 takes ownership and calls net_pkt_unref(); on error caller retains ownership.
